content.full_text:
July 27, 1976


Mr. Yoong Jun Fah
Embassy of Singapore
1824 R Street, N.W.
Washington, D.C. 20009

Dear Mr. Fah:

The Office of the Attorney General, State of California, has
forwarded your June 16, 1976 letter to this Commission for
response regarding registration of political parties.

The people of the State of California enacted the Political Reform
Act in 1974. The Act requires every person, or combination of
persons, who receive contributions totaling $500 or more in a
calendar year to file a Statement of Organization with the Secretary
of State within ten days after being formed as a committee.
(Sections 32013, 84104) A copy of the Political Reform Act, which
deals with disclosure by political entities, and an informational
packet are enclosed.

Please contact us if we can be of further assistance.

Sincerely,


RICHARD EICHMAN
Special Compliance Representative

RE:mak
Enclosures

cc: Mr. Dave D'Arco
Office of the Attorney General

bcc: Jeanne


State of California

Memorandum

To : Fair Political Practice's Commission
925 J St., Suite 522
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attention: Mr. Rich Eichman

From : Office of the Attorney General
Public Inquiry Unit, 555 Capitol Hall, Sacramento, CA 95314

Subject: Enclosed correspondence from the Embassy of Singapore

Department of Justice

Date : July 29, 1976

File No.: [illegible]

Here is a copy of the letter we discussed over the telephone requesting information on registration of political parties. Any information you might be able to send them, such as state or pamphlets, or indicate where they might be able to obtain the material they seek, would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

Dave D'Arco

Dave D'Arco


Cable Address: SINGAWAKIL WASHINGTON
Our Ref: SEM 390+570/4/13
Your Ref:

EMBASSY OF SINGAPORE,
1824 R STREET, N.W.,
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20009.
TEL: (202) 667-7555

16 June 1976

The Office of the Attorney
General
3580 Wilshire Boulevard
Los Angeles, Calif. 90010

Dear Sir,

LEGISLATIONS GOVERNING
SOCIETIES AND POLITICAL PARTIES

In Singapore, societies are registered under the Societies
Act as opposed to commercial organisations which are governed by the
Companies Act.

We are planning to revise our Societies Act and for this
purpose we are reviewing the provisions of similar legislations in
developed countries including the United States. We are informed
that such legislations are State legislations in this country. We
would be grateful if you could send us a copy of such legislations
of your State.

We would also be grateful if you could tell us whether
political parties are registered under such legislations. If they
are not, could you also send us a copy of the legislations governing
political parties.

Yours faithfully,

YOUNG JUN PAH
First Secretary (Commercial)

YJP/mla
